article_title: Jugular foramen tumor causing isolated hypoglossal nerve palsy: a case report
label: Figure 1
caption: Clinical photograph of the tongue showing leftward deviation upon protrusion, presenting as asymmetry.

article_title: Jugular foramen tumor causing isolated hypoglossal nerve palsy: a case report
label: Figure 2
caption: MRI findings. (a) Axial T2-weighted image shows a hyperintense nodule (long arrow) adjacent to the hypoglossal nerve (short arrow). (b) Axial contrast-enhanced T1-weighted image demonstrates ring-like enhancement of the nodule. (c) 3D contrast-enhanced T1-weighted axial reconstruction shows close contact between the extracranial hypoglossal nerve (short arrow) and the tumor, with a thin bony septum (long arrow) between the hypoglossal canal and jugular foramen. (d) 3D contrast-enhanced T1-weighted oblique sagittal reconstruction illustrates the intimate relationship between the tumor (long arrow) and the hypoglossal nerve (short arrow).

caption: Clinical timeline summarizing key events and interventions.

article_title: Cochlear Implantation for Sensorineural Hearing Loss Related to Cochlear Aqueduct Obstruction by a Jugular Foramen Tumor
label: FIGURE 2
caption: Axial contrast‐enhanced T1 MRI. Red arrow: Presence of enhancement of left cochlear aqueduct and adjacent dura consistent with tumor involvement.

article_title: Cochlear Implantation for Sensorineural Hearing Loss Related to Cochlear Aqueduct Obstruction by a Jugular Foramen Tumor
label: FIGURE 4
caption: T2 axial MRI images of jugular foramen tumor (A) pre‐SRS (3.0 mm slice thickness) and (B) 3 months post‐SRS (5.0 mm slice thickness) revealing changes in tumor size.

article_title: Cochlear Implantation for Sensorineural Hearing Loss Related to Cochlear Aqueduct Obstruction by a Jugular Foramen Tumor
label: FIGURE 1
caption: Axial heavily T2 weighted MRI. Blue arrow: Presence of fluid signal in cochlear aqueduct on non‐tumor size, indicating no occlusion. Red arrow: Absence of fluid signal in cochlear aqueduct related to tumor involvement.

article_title: Cochlear Implantation for Sensorineural Hearing Loss Related to Cochlear Aqueduct Obstruction by a Jugular Foramen Tumor
label: FIGURE 5
caption: T1 post‐contrast FLASH coronal MRI images of jugular foramen tumor (A) pre‐SRS (3.0 mm slice thickness) and (B) 3 months post‐SRS (5.0 mm slice thickness) revealing changes in tumor size.
